description: For teaching, lecturing, and education administration roles. Emphasises pedagogy, student outcomes, and commitment to education.

examples.title: Primary School Teacher Application
examples.body:
11 January 2026

Dear Head Teacher,

I am writing to apply for the teaching position at your school. I am a qualified teacher with a Primary Teachers' Diploma from Mufulira College of Education, and I am passionate about nurturing young minds and helping children build strong educational foundations.

My training covered child development, curriculum design, classroom management, and inclusive education. I have completed teaching practice at two primary schools, where I taught Mathematics and English to Grades 4 and 5. During my final teaching practice, I designed a remedial mathematics programme for struggling learners, using manipulatives and visual aids to make abstract concepts concrete. Seven of the ten learners who participated improved their test scores by at least 20%.

I believe that every child can learn, but not every child learns the same way. I am committed to differentiating instruction, using a mix of direct teaching, group work, and independent exploration to reach diverse learners. I am also passionate about reading, and I plan to establish a classroom library and daily reading time to foster literacy and a love of books.

I am a patient, creative, and organised teacher who believes that positive relationships are the foundation of effective teaching. I greet every child by name, celebrate their efforts as well as their achievements, and communicate regularly with parents.

I would be honoured to join your school community and to contribute to the growth and success of your learners.

Yours faithfully,

Beatrice Zulu

examples.title: Secondary School Science Teacher Application
examples.body:
14 February 2026

Dear Principal,

I am applying for the position of Science Teacher at your secondary school. I hold a Bachelor of Education in Natural Sciences from the University of Zambia, with a specialisation in Biology and Chemistry, and I am eager to inspire the next generation of Zambian scientists.

My teaching philosophy is that science should be experienced, not just memorised. During my teaching practice, I transformed a theoretical lesson on photosynthesis into a hands-on experiment where students grew bean seedlings under different light conditions and measured growth rates. The lesson increased student engagement dramatically, and the head teacher observed that students who had previously struggled with science concepts were asking questions and participating actively.

I am also committed to making science relevant to Zambian contexts. I use local examples, the chemistry of nshima fermentation, the biology of malaria transmission, the physics of bicycle mechanics, to show students that science is not abstract, it is all around them. I believe this approach increases motivation and helps students see science as a career pathway, not just a school subject.

Beyond the classroom, I am prepared to contribute to extracurricular activities, particularly a science club or science fair, where students can explore interests beyond the curriculum. I am also comfortable using technology, including projectors, educational software, and online resources, to enhance instruction.

I am available to start in January 2026 and willing to teach any science classes assigned to me.

Yours sincerely,

Henry Chomba

examples.title: Lecturer Application for Technical College
examples.body:
5 May 2026

Dear Principal,

I am writing to apply for the Lecturer position in the Business Studies Department at your technical college. I hold a Master of Business Administration and have six years of professional experience in accounting and finance, and I am excited about the opportunity to prepare students for careers in business.

My professional experience includes three years as an accountant at a manufacturing firm and three years as a financial analyst at a bank. I have prepared financial statements, conducted variance analysis, managed budgets, and advised management on investment decisions. This practical experience allows me to teach not just the theory, but the real-world application of accounting principles. When I teach ratio analysis, I bring actual financial statements I have worked with, anonymised, and show students how these ratios influence lending decisions and management strategy.

I am also passionate about pedagogy. I believe that technical college students need skills, not just knowledge. I design my lessons around competencies, what should a student be able to do after this lesson? I use case studies, simulations, and group projects to build practical skills. I also maintain industry connections, bringing guest speakers and arranging factory visits so students understand where their skills will be applied.

I am committed to my own professional development and hold an ongoing practising certificate with the Zambia Institute of Chartered Accountants. I am also pursuing a postgraduate diploma in Education to strengthen my teaching practice.

I would welcome the opportunity to contribute to your college's mission of producing skilled, employable graduates.

Yours sincerely,

Catherine Musonda
